On-Screen Text / Copy:
Hook: Aesthetic practices are doing influencer marketing wrong
and it's costing them bookings.

Slide 2: Here's what they do:
Gift a free treatment. Get one feed post, maybe even just one story post. No tracking. No commission. No follow-up.
Then decide influencer marketing "is not needed”

Slide 3: Here's why that kills the whole strategy:
When you run it that way, you strip out the one thing that makes it work.
Clients browsing aesthetic services are deciding whether to trust someone with their face and body. That decision heightens when it comes from someone they already know and trust. If that person shows them the full experience, walk-in to results, and makes it feel safe, why wouldn't they book?
That's the only thing influencer marketing does that paid ads can't.

Slide 4: 
And that's not the only thing you're missing out on.
Followers comment and DM influencers with the questions they'd never ask a clinic. "Did it hurt? Was it worth it?" Conversations begin happening around your brand.
The influencer answers. Objections get handled. Not only are you getting direct bookings and a major increase in warm leads, you're also building your community within someone else's.

Slide 5: Here's how influencer marketing should actually work for your practice:
Wrong: chasing follower count. Right: chasing local relevance.
A creator with 6K followers where most of them are in your city will out-book one with 80K spread across 12 states every time. Their audience has to be able to walk through your door. If they can't, the post means nothing.
Slide 6: Wrong: one post and done. Right: building an ongoing relationship.
One post sparks curiosity. Repeated content builds trust. Trust is what converts. The influencer who showed up for your practice three times over two months will always out-book the one who posted once and moved on.
Slide 7: Wrong: chasing a big audience for one great post. Right: finding someone whose lifestyle your practice already fits into.
One post gets forgotten. But a creator who genuinely lives the aesthetic lifestyle, who your practice fits naturally into their content, their routine, their brand, keeps selling for you without it ever feeling like a sell.
Their audience doesn't see a promotion. They see a life they want. And your practice is already part of it.

Slide 8: Wrong: gifting a treatment and hoping they post. Right: tying their comp to actual bookings.
"For every $250 gift card sold through your code, you earn $50." That one shift changes everything. Now they're posting more than once, answering every DM, and closing for you. They have skin in the game.

Slide 9: Wrong: no tracking. Right: every influencer gets a unique promo code and their own landing page.
If you can't trace a booking back to a specific influencer, you don't have a campaign. You have a guess. And you can't scale a guess.

Slide 10:
Save this.
Follow for more strategy that actually fills schedules.
Caption Hook/Info:
If you haven't been using influencer marketing for your practice, you should be. And if you have and it hasn't been working, this is exactly why.
Gifting a free treatment and hoping for a post isn't a strategy. But this is:
Chase local relevance over follower count
Build ongoing relationships, not one-off campaigns
Find creators whose lifestyle your practice already fits into
Tie their comp to actual bookings
Track every booking back to a source
Save this for when you're ready to do it right. 

#medspamarketing #aestheticmarketing #medspaowner #medspabusiness #medspagrowth

On-Screen Text / Copy:
SLIDE 1 — HOOK To the aesthetic practice owner who's tired of paying for marketing that doesn't bring results.

SLIDE 2 — CREDIBILITY 15 years. Over $1 billion in aesthetic revenue generated.
Here's what we've learned about why marketing fails for practices like yours.

SLIDE 3 — PROBLEM: VANITY METRICS / NO TRACKING They showed you a report every month full of numbers going up.
What they never showed you was how many of those people actually booked. Because they didn't know. And neither did you.
Impressions, reach, and follower counts mean nothing if they're not connected to real tracking. Without it, you're flying blind — and paying for it.

SLIDE 4 — SOLUTION: TRACKING The fix: connect every marketing channel to real numbers.
Track CPL so you know what you're paying per lead
Track show rate so you know who's actually walking in
Track CPA so you know what a booked patient costs you
Build a dashboard that ties ad spend directly to revenue
When you can see where leads come from and where they drop off, you stop wasting money and start making decisions that grow your practice.

SLIDE 5 — PROBLEM: WRONG PATIENT TARGETING You kept getting leads who ghosted, negotiated your prices, or booked once and never came back.
That's not bad luck. That's what happens when your marketing has no clear patient avatar. You were reaching everyone — and converting no one.
The right strategy attracts the right patients and filters out the wrong ones.

SLIDE 6 — SOLUTION: PATIENT TARGETING Start by defining exactly who your best patient is.
What treatments do they book repeatedly?
What is their average spend per visit?
Where do they find you — Google, Instagram, referrals?
What do they say when they describe their problem?
Every ad, caption, and offer gets built around that person. You stop attracting price shoppers and start attracting patients who book without hesitation and come back.

SLIDE 7 — PROBLEM: NO FRONT DESK TRAINING Here's the part nobody talks about.
You can have the best ads, the best SEO, and a fully optimized website. But if the person answering your phones doesn't know how to convert a curious caller into a booked patient — it all leaks out at the last step.
Most agencies hand you leads and walk away. Nobody trains the person actually closing them.

SLIDE 8 — SOLUTION: FRONT DESK TRAINING Your front desk is your highest-converting touchpoint — treat it like one.
Train your team to ask questions, not just answer them
Script responses to the most common objections
Set a response time standard — 5 minutes or less
Track how many calls turn into consultations
The best marketing means nothing if the person on the phone isn't equipped to close.

SLIDE 9 — CTA This is what a real marketing partner looks at.
Not just your ads. Not just your traffic. But your tracking, your targeting, and your team.
Notes: Speaks directly to your target audiences pain point, these generally get more followers.
Caption Hook/Info:
If you've hired marketing help before and it didn't work — this isn't on you.
Most agencies focus on one piece of the puzzle and call it done. No real tracking. No patient targeting strategy. No support for the team converting those leads into bookings.
After 15 years and over $1 billion in aesthetic revenue generated, we know exactly where the gaps are and how to close them.
Our DMs are always open!
#medspamarketing #medspaowner #aestheticmarketing #medspagrowth #medspabusiness
